summary: Round-3 cycle addressing external review by gndrmnn: (1) rewrote the secretToKeystoneMapper doc comment in keystone_controller.go to describe only current behavior — removed the paragraph that framed the index path as a replacement for a 'prior unfiltered namespace-scoped List' and dropped the 'CC-0087 review #1' parenthetical on owner-ref scoping; (2) removed the tautological assertion g.Expect(reqs[0].NamespacedName.Name).To(Equal(ks.Name)) from TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnerRefGroupMatchIgnoresVersion, keeping only the HaveLen(1) behavioral check. Both changes are mechanical, in-scope, and faithfully implement the reviewer's resolution text. Two review-pattern files were updated/added to capture the lessons (no-historical-references-in-comments; reject-tautological-constant-tests occurrence bumped 2→3). The progress manifest phase flipped awaiting_external_review → improving. No logic, control flow, or test coverage was changed — the mapper's index lookup, owner-ref fallback, dedup, and cached Get behavior remain intact.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (pre-review gates: 0 golangci-lint issues, 0 go vet issues, gosec clean, no stubs)
Edge cases are covered (index path, owner-ref path, group-match-ignores-version, different-group-ignored, dedup, error swallow — all pre-existing tests retained)

Removed assertion was tautological (compared ks.Name against the very ks.Name wired into the owner-ref built two lines earlier); removal does not weaken coverage — HaveLen(1) still proves the group-match-ignores-version invariant
No test was added or deleted; only one line removed from TestSecretToKeystoneMapper_OwnerRefGroupMatchIgnoresVersion

code_quality_checklist.item: Doc comment now describes current behavior only (index-backed name lookup + owner-ref fallback with cached Get) — no reference to prior List implementation
Requirement/feature-ID footprint preserved: REQ-001, REQ-002, REQ-003, REQ-005, CC-0087 still cited in the doc comment (satisfies FEATURE_ID_REQUIRED rule)
Naming, formatting, and structure unchanged — pure comment/assertion pruning
No dead code, no TODOs, no commented-out code introduced

security_checklist.item: No authz/validation/boundary logic touched — changes are limited to comment text and one test assertion
gosec clean on the two changed Go files

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is minimal and targeted — two surgical edits that exactly match the external reviewer's resolution text
No new abstractions, APIs, or patterns introduced
Mapper behavior (List with MatchingFields{KeystoneSecretNameIndexKey}, owner-ref group/kind filter, cached Get, dedup by NamespacedName) is unchanged

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Tautological assertion removed — aligns with reject-tautological-constant-tests pattern

fail_fast_checklist.item: SetupWithManager still returns indexer registration errors; indexer failure path covered by TestIntegration_IndexerRegistrationFailsManagerStartCleanly (unchanged)

defensive_checklist.item: Owner-ref cached Get still guards against stale refs; non-NotFound errors still fall through to enqueue (behavior unchanged)

suggested_improvements: Consistency follow-up (out of this diff's scope, NOT a finding): keystone_controller.go:616 and keystone_controller_test.go:2766,2798 still contain '(CC-0087 review #1)' parentheticals. The newly-filed no-historical-references-in-comments pattern would call for removing these in a future sweep — but they were NOT introduced by this diff and the review-scope-restriction prohibits flagging pre-existing code. Leaving as an informational note only.
next_steps: Proceed to next phase — no blocking or major findings.

Optionally, in a follow-up hygiene PR, scrub remaining 'review #1' parentheticals in keystone_controller.go:616 and keystone_controller_test.go:2766,2798 to fully apply the no-historical-references-in-comments pattern file-wide.
